Author: Team Wrath
Name: Wrath Combo
Punchline: Condenses combos and mutually exclusive abilities onto a single button - and then some.
Description: Turns one action into many! Similar to how PvP actions work, we take an action and build a whole combo out of it. Sometimes it's as simple as a 1-2-3 combo, other times it's a full job rotation. Either way, you'll find something suited for your rotation needs.
Changelog: Please check the project's GitHub or Discord for a full changelog.

Author: daemitus, kawaii
Name: Yes Already
Punchline: Clicks Yes in various dialogs you've specified.
Description: Tired of clicking yes in Praetorium? Can't stand using keys in dungeons? Well do I have the plugin for you! Yes Already is specifically designed to click all the annoying dialogs you've grown accustomed facerolling. Just enter the dialog text, and you'll never see it again! Also works with /regex/ and has several built-in modules for dialogs that aren't quite yes/no prompts but act like it (like desynth).

Author: Chalkos, NightmareXIV
Name: Marketbuddy
Punchline: Less clicks, more gil!
Description: Helper for your day-to-day market operations.

Author: NightmareXIV
Name: Splatoon
Punchline: Scriptable overlay engine for drawing mechanics, markers, and indicators in real-time
Description:
Splatoon is a scriptable telegraph and overlay engine for Final Fantasy XIV, built on the Dalamud plugin framework. It allows scripts to draw shapes, markers, labels, and indicators directly in the game world. These overlays can be attached to actors, positions, or events, and are rendered with precise positioning.

Splatoon does not include presets or encounter logic by default. If you want presets, you must create them yourself or import them from others.

In short, Splatoon draws things. What it draws is up to you!
Changelog: - Added No draw checkbox to layouts

Author: Liza Carvelli & various contributors
Name: Questionable
Punchline: A tiny quest helper plugin.
Description:
A small quest helper plugin designed to automatically do your quests for you where possible. Utilizes navmesh to automatically travel to all quest waypoints and (attempts to) complete all steps along the way (excluding dungeons, single-player duties and combat).

Not all quests are supported.

Required Plugins: vnavmesh, TextAdvance, Lifestream
Changelog: - Bug fix: avoid overriding aethernet shortcuts if they're set -alydev

Author: Taurenkey, kawaii, NightmareXIV
Name: Artisan
Punchline: A 'simple' crafting plugin.
Description: The all-in-one crafting plugin. Perfect for people with next to no knowledge about crafting rotations. No need for a library of different macros, the solvers will work dynamically to try and complete your crafts. Also provides convenient ways to craft lists of items or grind out a specific item repeatedly.
Changelog:
- Fix issue with graphical simulator not updating Raphael solutions correctly.
- Try and prevent adjusting Raphael macros to skip Excellent GS/BB combo when durability would cause a fail.

fix deck rebuilding when rules changed.

Author: Haselnussbomber
Name: Yo-kai Check
Description: Yo-kai Check is a small plugin to assist you in tracking the rewards of the Yo-kai Watch event.
Punchline: Tracking assistant for Yo-kai Watch rewards
Changelog:
- Added: The server info bar entry can not be clicked to open the plugin window.
- Added: The server info bar entry now shows the locations in which you can farm the medals in the tooltip.
- Added: In the table, the medal count of each weapon now also has a tooltip that shows the locations in which you can farm them. A location displayed in a green color is your current location.
- Added: In the table, the minion name can now be clicked to summon it and will be shown in a green color when it is currently summoned.
- Changed: The server info bar entry is now based on the currently summoned minion (previously it was based on inventory changes - the medals that were added) and will persist between zones until the minion is sent away or the weapon has been acquired.

Name: Auto Login
Description: Automatically logs you into a specified character when the game first opens, and optionally reconnects you to the game (and notifies you) when you get disconnected.
Changelog: - Updated for 7.5

Author: Aida Enna
Name: Auto Pillion
Punchline: Automatically ride on other people's mounts!
Description:
When a party member gets on a mount that supports more than 1 person and is close enough, you will automatically get on.

This plugin is in testing and will have one or more issues, laid out below:

- You may need to right click on the driver once each time you start the game before the plugin will automount their mount. A fix is being investigated.

Please report issues/bugs and post suggestions on the Github website by clicking the globe icon.
Changelog: - Updated for patch 7.5

Author: Aida Enna
Name: Macro Chain Redux
Description:
Allows for a macro to continue onto the next macro by using '/nextmacro' on the last line of a macro.
Adding '/nextmacro' on the last line of Macro #01 will automatically execute Macro #02
Adding '/nextmacro down' on the last line of Macro #01 will automatically execute Macro #11
Also allows executing any macro from chat using '/runmacro'
You can repeat a macro by using /runmacro inside the macro followed by the number of the macro you're editing
Using '/macrocancel' will stop the currently running macro
Punchline: A modified fork of the old Macro Chain that allows you to run multiple macros one after the other (or repeat them).
Changelog: - Updated for patch 7.5

Author: Aida Enna
Name: Portrait Fixer
Description:
When you click "Update Gear Set", this plugin will automatically update and save your portait to match your new glam.

This plugin is in testing and will have one or more issues, laid out below:

- Rarely, a glitched desynth window will pop up when saving the gearset instead of it saving the portait. This is being investigated, closing the window and swapping classes seems to fix it.

Please report issues/bugs and post suggestions on the Github website by clicking the globe icon.
Punchline: Automatically update your portait when your gearset gets updated
Changelog: - Updated for patch 7.5

Author: attick, daemitus, alymphocyte, grammernatzi
Name: XIV Combo Expandedest
Punchline: Condenses combos and mutually exclusive abilities onto a single button. Plus extras.
Description:
This is an enhanced version of XIVCombo with a variety of new added features.
This is mutually exclusive with other XIVCombo versions, so make sure they do not conflict!
Changelog:
New features unlikely to be added until Evercold releases. Bug fixes will still happen, though.
    
    1.5.0.91: 
    * Removed Burst Strike/Fated Circle to Bloodfest as you now cannot overcap with Bloodfest.
    * Added Bloodfest to No Mercy.
    * Fixed a bug where Blizzard/Fire switcher did not work without Enochian unlocked.

    1.5.0.90: 
    * Updated to 7.4.
    * Some more changes coming soon but emergency maintenance started RIP
    
    1.5.0.82:
    * Reawakened Fangs Feature now also applies to Steel Maw.
    
    1.5.0.79:
    * Phlegma into Eukrasian Dyskrasia added.
    
    1.5.0.73:
    * Meisui/Tenri priority fixed and DWaD to Meisui added.
    * Monk's Draconic Leap feature now properly syncs below level 50 when you don't have a form.
    * Raise Hard Target Lockout Feature added.
    
    1.5.0.72:
    * Double Down features now account for its new cost.
    * New option for Royal Authority to Atonement where it will only activate if you are about to overwrite Sword Oath in your combo.
    * Empyreal Arrow will no longer turn into Pitch Perfect under any circumstances.
    
    1.5.0.71:
    * Updated for 7.1 compatibility.
    * Devilment to Starfall Dance feature removed as it is now vanilla.
    * Removed Jump to Mirage Dive due to it being vanilla, as I completely forgot to earlier.
    * Added an 'Only When Full' option to Sidewinder to Pitch Perfect.

    1.5.0.70:
    * Added Comet Overwrite Prevention feature. Or did I?
    * Removed Holy overcap features (it made no sense when you gain, not lose, DPS by letting it overcap).
    
    1.5.0.69 (Nice):
    * Sacrificium Feature added.
    * Despair to Flare Star added.
    * Dragon Kick to Forbidden Chakra added.
    
    1.5.0.67:
    * Blizzard 3 & 2 to Flare Star feature added.
    
    1.5.0.61:
    * Lucid Dreaming Reminders now have a configurable MP threshold, as well as a configurable cooldown remaining offset where they stop replacing the cooldown.
    
    1.5.0.60:
    * Combos to Saber Dance during Devilment feature added.
    * Some other stuff added to Dancer, too. Maybe.
    * Phlegma to Psyche added.
    
    1.5.0.59:
    * Isle Sprint feature added.
    
    1.5.0.53:
    * Reaving Steel added from regular XIVComboExpanded. Maybe something else, too? Tis a secret.
    * Hooking Hooksets and Chummy Cast added.
    
    1.5.0.51:
    * Chaotic Glide added.
    
    1.5.0.49:
    * Standard Step into Technical Step added.
    
    1.5.0.41:
    * Viper features added. Please look forward to it (happening right now).

Author: The Combat Reborn Team
Name: Rotation Solver Reborn
Description:
Analyses combat information every frame and finds the best action.

This means almost all the information available in one frame in combat, including the status of all players in the party, the status of any hostile targets, skill cooldowns, the MP and HP of characters, the location of characters, casting status of the hostile target, combo, combat duration, player level, etc.
Then, it will highlight the best action on the hot bar, or help you to click on it.
It is designed for `general combat`, not for savage or ultimate. Use it carefully.
Punchline: Analyses combat information every frame and finds the best action.
Changelog:
## What's Changed
* Fix DNC esprit thresholds, countdown opener, and Showtime window logic by @aventurescence in https://github.com/FFXIV-CombatReborn/RotationSolverReborn/pull/1291


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/FFXIV-CombatReborn/RotationSolverReborn/compare/7.5.1.2...7.5.1.3

Author: Anna
Changelog:
- Slightly redesign the manager tab
- Add ability to search for already-installed mods on Heliosphere and convert them
- Add IPC event for mod conversions
- Update dependencies
Description:
Heliosphere is a mod manager and platform designed to work directly with
Penumbra. It enables efficient downloads and fast installs to let you get back
to the fun part of mods: using them.

Heliosphere supports:
- Direct install to Penumbra
- Compressed and deduplicated (read: small) downloads
- Automatic updates (optional)
- Version history
- Mod changelogs
- Pictures in Penumbra
- And more

Give Heliosphere a try, and see if you can't say goodbye to any old mod
platform you were using before.
Name: Heliosphere
Punchline: An efficient mod manager and platform that integrates directly with Penumbra.
TestingChangelog:
- Fix a possible error in the multiple directory warning
- Fix updates for mods with duplicated files
- Add a first-time-user setup window to make onboarding easier

Author: Valenth Siveria
Name: I Am Ready
Punchline: Automatically accept or decline Duty Finder ready checks based on a configurable counter.
Description:
# I Am Ready

**I Am Ready** is a Dalamud plugin for Final Fantasy XIV that automatically accepts or declines Party and Alliance Ready Checks based on a customizable counter. 

Whether you are stepping away for a moment or just tired of clicking, this plugin handles ready checks for you while mimicking human reaction times.

## ✨ Features

* **Auto-Answer Ready Checks:** Automatically clicks "Yes" or "No" when a ready check appears.
* **Configurable "Yes" Counter:** Set a specific number of times you want to automatically answer "Yes". Once that limit is reached, the plugin will automatically start answering "No" to prevent pulling when you are actually away.
* **Natural Delay:** Enable a randomized delay (0.7 to 2.5 seconds) before the plugin answers, making it look like a natural human reaction.
* **Smart Chat Triggers (Regex):** Add custom text phrases to the plugin. If someone says one of these phrases in Party or Alliance chat (e.g., "reset", "pulling"), your counter will automatically reset back to zero.
* **Bilingual Support:** The user interface is available in both English and German.

## 🚀 Usage

1. Type `/iar` in the in-game chat to open the configuration window.
2. Click the **Inactive/Active** button at the top to turn the auto-responder on or off.
3. Adjust the **Yes Count** slider. For example, if set to `5`, the plugin will answer "Yes" to the next 5 ready checks, and "No" to any checks after that.
4. Click the **Start** button at any time to manually reset your current counter back to zero.

### Chat Triggers (Regex Patterns)
At the bottom of the window, you can expand the **Regex Patterns** section. Here you can add simple words or complex regex patterns. 
If the plugin reads a matching message in your Party or Cross-World Party chat, it will instantly reset your counter to zero, assuming a new pull or instance is starting!

/iar - opens the I Am Ready tool
Changelog: API15 finally

- No more 0 cards in Auto Mode when Player Roll Mode is enabled: the game now waits for the active player's valid 13-sided roll

NEW:

- Per-player choices are saved persistently

Description: Reads your Doman Mahjong hand and either suggests the best discard or plays the match for you.
Changelog: v0.1.2.3: Fix the in-game suggestion highlight after a call. Once you called pon, chi, or kan, the highlight could jump to the wall along the top of the board or land on the wrong tile, because it located your hand by geometry and the shrunken hand let the perfectly-aligned wall slots win. It now anchors to the bottom-most row (your hand is always at the bottom) and ignores melded tiles, so the outline stays on the suggested discard. The hand reading and suggestion were always correct; only the on-tile outline was affected.

Description: Lists every armoire-eligible dungeon drop in the game and shows whether each is in your inventory, in your armoire, or still missing. Sort by level, hide completed dungeons, toggle to show owned items too. Open the armoire UI at an inn once per session to populate the in-armoire state; in-inventory and equipped state is detected automatically. Use /armoire.

Description: Gluttony Combo - lalalazy fork of Gluttony Combo. XIVCombo for very lazy players.
Changelog:
v1.0.4.54 - Upstream sync: WrathCombo main 1.0.4.9 tip (3 commits: autorotation override-target handling)
- Merged upstream 06877cca..1c7049c64; all Gluttony fork divergences preserved (15s raidwide-mit gate, Pyretic/Pacification/Silence handling, WHM Divine Caress ground-heal, BLU engine, SMN Aegis Uptime).
- Autorotation: the override target is now cleared automatically when it points at a dead/invalid object, and wiped when autorotation is disabled, instead of being nulled at every early-exit. Fewer stale-target edge cases.
- Autorotation: ability queueing now respects the configured queue window (AnimationLock <= QueueWindow) instead of requiring a zero animation lock, so weaved oGCDs fire more reliably.
- Target: HasBattleTarget() is now null-safe and the override-target getter self-wipes dead targets.

Author: daemitus, MidoriKami, dajoey
Name: Lazy WT Math
Punchline: FFXIV Wondrous Tails probability calculator.
Description: Adds row probabilities to the Wondrous Tails display along with the average probability of what would happen if you shuffled. Upgraded for modern FFXIV.
Changelog:
v3.2.2.8 - Upstream ezwt Features
- Ported EzWondrousTails v3.2.2.6 probability updates and bingo UI.
- Preserved Dalamud API 15 / .NET 10 upgrades and unmanaged pointer detours in KamiToolKit.

Author: Blackcatz1911, dajoey
Name: Lazy Currency Spender
Punchline: Find the absolute best way to spend all your FFXIV currencies.
Description: Fork of CurrencySpender by Blackcatz1911. Never wonder 'What should I do with this?' again. Access real-time prices from Universalis, identify missing collectibles tied to your currencies, and unlock new possibilities for utilizing your in-game resources effectively.
Changelog:
v1.2.6.1 - Equipment Rendering Support
- Added new 'Equipment and Gear Exchange' section in the spending UI to display untradable, non-collectable equipment (such as Bygone Brass gear and weapons) purchased with weekly capped/uncapped endgame tomestones.

Author: croizat, dajoey
Name: Lazy Fate Automation
Punchline: Fully automated FATE grinding utilizing vnavmesh, lifestream, and Gluttony Combo.
Description: Automates FATE grinding, including Zodiac Atma, Anima Luminous Crystals, Resistance Memories, Phantom Demiatmas, Yo-kai Watch Medals, and Bicolor Gemstones. Seamlessly integrates with vnavmesh for pathing and flight, lifestream for zone swapping, TextAdvance for dialogue skipping, and Gluttony Combo/BossMod for combat. Use /lazyfate to open.
Changelog:
v0.0.1.38 - 2026-06-09
- Prevented mounting and dismounting loops after FATE completion by keeping the bot in Engaging state (clearing remaining combat) before transitioning to BetweenFates or deactivating integrations.

v0.0.1.35 - 2026-06-07
- Stop movement and remove dismount/landing logic from `TeleportTo` pre-cast checks. Teleport casts will now initiate directly while remaining mounted.
- Exclude city hubs (where mounting is disabled) from random zone swapping, gemstone allowed zones, relic allowed zones, and the UI zone selector.

v0.0.1.34 - 2026-06-07
- Added stuck check mitigation for combat/engage pathfinding fallbacks.
- Wait for combat to end before mounting.

v0.0.1.33 - 2026-06-07
- Only dismount before teleporting if the player is flying (`Player.InFlight` is true). If the player is mounted on the ground, they will now remain mounted while teleporting, which makes travels faster and more natural.
- Added robust mounting verification and retry loops inside `MoveTo()`. If the player is in combat, the bot stands still and waits for combat to end before attempting to mount.
- Added mid-travel dismount checking inside `MoveTo()` pathfinding loops. If the player is dismounted mid-travel (e.g. from getting aggroed/hit), the bot halts movement, waits for combat to end, mounts up, and resumes pathfinding rather than walking on foot.
- Rewrote combat/engage stuck detection in `HandleCombatStuckDetection()`. Instead of depending on `InCombat` (which is false when running between FATE mobs) or `IsMoving` (which returns false when stuck against a wall), it now tracks position changes relative to the current target and activates `vnavmesh` pathing fallback if progress towards a distant target stops for 1.5 seconds.
- Added explicit landing and dismounting calls at the beginning of `TeleportTo()`, and wait for `!Player.IsBusy` to prevent teleport casts from immediately failing when mounted/flying.
- Fixed task crashing on teleport failures by replacing `ErrorIf(!ActionManager.Teleport())` with a robust 3-attempt retry loop that falls back to the `/return` recovery gracefully.

v0.0.1.26 - 2026-06-07
- Added combat stuck detection and mitigation in `HandleCombatStuckDetection()`. If BossMod's straight-line movement gets the player stuck on trees/obstacles in combat for 1.5 seconds, the bot disables BossMod movement and uses `vnavmesh` to pathfind around the obstacle to the target.
- Added auto-skipping for NPC dialogue SelectString option lists in `TaskBase.WaitUntilSkipping()`.
- Gated teleporting and mounting on `!Svc.Condition[ConditionFlag.InCombat]` in `TaskBase.cs` to prevent getting stuck in combat.
- Prevented rapid mounting and dismounting loops during chain FATEs while waiting for the next FATE to spawn.

v0.0.1.23 - 2026-06-06
- File logging now suppresses DBG/TRC scope tracing by default; only WRN/ERR are written to LazyFateAutomation.log. Set VerboseFileLogging to true in the plugin config (LazyFateAutomation.json) to restore full debug logging for troubleshooting. (Svc.cs LogToFile gate, Configuration.cs flag.)
- Fixes ~28 MB log growth observed 2026-06-06 from FATE-grind DebugContext scope enter/exit tracing.

Author: dajoey
Name: Lazy Skyward Tracker
Punchline: Track your Skyward points for all jobs toward the Pteranodon mount
Description: Tracks all jobs' Skyward points for the Pteranodon mount achievement. Scans your inventory for Skybuilders turn-in items and projects how many points they'll add, shown as a cyan bar extension on each job's progress bar. Use /lazysky to open.

Author: dajoey
Name: LazyFoodBuff
Punchline: Never forget to eat your food again.
Description: Auto-eats food in combat duties with per-job food selection, auto-select based on best stats, and a low-food warning. Fires in combat duties (dungeons, raids, trials, alliance raids, criterion, variant, deep dungeons). Understands optimal stats for every job and auto-selects the best food from your inventory.
Changelog:
v0.1.1.0 - Low-food warning + deep dungeons. (1) The low-stock alert now triggers on how much food you have left in your bag, not on time remaining on the buff; new count slider (default 3), alerts once and re-arms after you restock. (2) Auto-eat now works in deep dungeons (Palace of the Dead, Heaven-on-High, Eureka Orthos) - they were wrongly excluded.
v0.1.0.0 - Initial release. Auto-eats food in combat duties; per-job food selection or auto-select by best stats; refresh-before-expiry. Use /lazyfoodbuff.

Author: Sevii
Description: Auto updates mods and allows for more customization such as color and compositing onto other files
Name: Aetherment
Punchline: Auto-updating and more customizable mods
